summary: #90154 is the only hydrated open PR and is the canonical repair target for this shard. It is useful, focused on gateway auth behavior, maintainer-modifiable, and not security-sensitive in the preflight artifact, but it is not merge-ready because checks were unavailable, mergeability is unknown, comments/review-bot comments were not hydrated, and there is no Codex /review proof. Plan a contributor-branch repair/finalization pass rather than merge or replacement.

actions.reason: Useful contributor PR should be finalized by repairing/revalidating the contributor branch, preserving coygeek's authorship, before any merge recommendation.

repair_candidate.summary: Repair and finalize contributor PR #90154 rather than replacing it. Re-fetch current PR state, hydrate the one issue comment and any review-bot comments, rebase or refresh the contributor branch if stale, run the gateway auth validation lane plus pnpm check:changed, run Codex /review, address findings narrowly, then emit merge_canonical only if the full merge preflight is clean.
repair_candidate.pr_title: fix(gateway): restrict backend self-pairing shared auth
repair_candidate.pr_body: Finalize contributor PR #90154 by repairing the existing branch, not replacing it by default. This carries forward coygeek's implementation that removes shared gateway token/password auth from the direct-local backend self-pairing exception while preserving dedicated backend paths. Before merge, hydrate comments and bot review comments, rebase or refresh against current main, run targeted gateway auth tests plus pnpm check:changed, run Codex /review, and address any findings. Source PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/90154. Linked issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/72418. Historical context: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/72419.
credit_notes: Preserve coygeek as the source PR author and retain commit attribution where possible.

Any final merge or replacement rationale should credit https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/90154 as the source PR and mention that coygeek's contribution carried the gateway auth restriction forward.
